Detailed Description

Miscellaneous functions for sending an email.

Authors
  • Michael R. Elkins
  • Pietro Cerutti

bool  istext

Encode text as quoted printable.

Parameters
fcCursor for converting a file's encoding
fp_outFile to store the result
istextIs the input text?

Definition at line 155 of file sendlib.c.

156 {
157  int c, linelen = 0;
158  char line[77], savechar;
159 
160  while ((c = mutt_ch_fgetconv(fc)) != EOF)
161  {
162  /* Wrap the line if needed. */
163  if ((linelen == 76) && ((istext && (c != '\n')) || !istext))
164  {
165  /* If the last character is "quoted", then be sure to move all three
166  * characters to the next line. Otherwise, just move the last
167  * character... */
168  if (line[linelen - 3] == '=')
169  {
170  line[linelen - 3] = 0;
171  fputs(line, fp_out);
172  fputs("=\n", fp_out);
173  line[linelen] = 0;
174  line[0] = '=';
175  line[1] = line[linelen - 2];
176  line[2] = line[linelen - 1];
177  linelen = 3;
178  }
179  else
180  {
181  savechar = line[linelen - 1];
182  line[linelen - 1] = '=';
183  line[linelen] = 0;
184  fputs(line, fp_out);
185  fputc('\n', fp_out);
186  line[0] = savechar;
187  linelen = 1;
188  }
189  }
190 
191  /* Escape lines that begin with/only contain "the message separator". */
192  if ((linelen == 4) && mutt_str_startswith(line, "From", CASE_MATCH))
193  {
194  mutt_str_strfcpy(line, "=46rom", sizeof(line));
195  linelen = 6;
196  }
197  else if ((linelen == 4) && mutt_str_startswith(line, "from", CASE_MATCH))
198  {
199  mutt_str_strfcpy(line, "=66rom", sizeof(line));
200  linelen = 6;
201  }
202  else if ((linelen == 1) && (line[0] == '.'))
203  {
204  mutt_str_strfcpy(line, "=2E", sizeof(line));
205  linelen = 3;
206  }
207 
208  if ((c == '\n') && istext)
209  {
210  /* Check to make sure there is no trailing space on this line. */
211  if ((linelen > 0) && ((line[linelen - 1] == ' ') || (line[linelen - 1] == '\t')))
212  {
213  if (linelen < 74)
214  {
215  sprintf(line + linelen - 1, "=%2.2X", (unsigned char) line[linelen - 1]);
216  fputs(line, fp_out);
217  }
218  else
219  {
220  int savechar2 = line[linelen - 1];
221 
222  line[linelen - 1] = '=';
223  line[linelen] = 0;
224  fputs(line, fp_out);
225  fprintf(fp_out, "\n=%2.2X", (unsigned char) savechar2);
226  }
227  }
228  else
229  {
230  line[linelen] = 0;
231  fputs(line, fp_out);
232  }
233  fputc('\n', fp_out);
234  linelen = 0;
235  }
236  else if ((c != 9) && ((c < 32) || (c > 126) || (c == '=')))
237  {
238  /* Check to make sure there is enough room for the quoted character.
239  * If not, wrap to the next line. */
240  if (linelen > 73)
241  {
242  line[linelen++] = '=';
243  line[linelen] = 0;
244  fputs(line, fp_out);
245  fputc('\n', fp_out);
246  linelen = 0;
247  }
248  sprintf(line + linelen, "=%2.2X", (unsigned char) c);
249  linelen += 3;
250  }
251  else
252  {
253  /* Don't worry about wrapping the line here. That will happen during
254  * the next iteration when I'll also know what the next character is. */
255  line[linelen++] = c;
256  }
257  }
258 
259  /* Take care of anything left in the buffer */
260  if (linelen > 0)
261  {
262  if ((line[linelen - 1] == ' ') || (line[linelen - 1] == '\t'))
263  {
264  /* take care of trailing whitespace */
265  if (linelen < 74)
266  sprintf(line + linelen - 1, "=%2.2X", (unsigned char) line[linelen - 1]);
267  else
268  {
269  savechar = line[linelen - 1];
270  line[linelen - 1] = '=';
271  line[linelen] = 0;
272  fputs(line, fp_out);
273  fputc('\n', fp_out);
274  sprintf(line, "=%2.2X", (unsigned char) savechar);
275  }
276  }
277  else
278  line[linelen] = 0;
279  fputs(line, fp_out);
280  }
281 }

Definition at line 302 of file sendlib.c.

303 {
304  /* for some reasons, mutt_b64_encode expects the
305  * output buffer to be larger than 10B */
306  char encoded[11];
307  size_t ret;
308 
309  if (bctx->size == 0)
310  return;
311 
312  if (bctx->linelen >= 72)
313  {
314  fputc('\n', fp_out);
315  bctx->linelen = 0;
316  }
317 
318  /* ret should always be equal to 4 here, because bctx->size
319  * is a value between 1 and 3 (included), but let's not hardcode it
320  * and prefer the return value of the function */
321  ret = mutt_b64_encode(bctx->buffer, bctx->size, encoded, sizeof(encoded));
322  for (size_t i = 0; i < ret; i++)
323  {
324  fputc(encoded[i], fp_out);
325  bctx->linelen++;
326  }
327 
328  bctx->size = 0;
329 }

352 {
353  struct B64Context bctx;
354  int ch, ch1 = EOF;
355 
356  b64_init(&bctx);
357 
358  while ((ch = mutt_ch_fgetconv(fc)) != EOF)
359  {
360  if (SigInt == 1)
361  {
362  SigInt = 0;
363  return;
364  }

368  ch1 = ch;
369  }
370  b64_flush(&bctx, fp_out);
371  fputc('\n', fp_out);
372 }

◆ mutt_write_mime_header()

int mutt_write_mime_header ( struct Body *  a,
FILE *  fp 
)

Create a MIME header.

Parameters
aBody part
fpFile to write to
Return values
0Success
-1Failure

Definition at line 401 of file sendlib.c.

402 {
403  if (!a || !fp)
404  return -1;
405 
406  int len;
407  int tmplen;
408  char buf[256] = { 0 };
409 
410  fprintf(fp, "Content-Type: %s/%s", TYPE(a), a->subtype);
411 
412  if (!TAILQ_EMPTY(&a->parameter))
413  {
414  len = 25 + mutt_str_strlen(a->subtype); /* approximate len. of content-type */
415 
416  struct Parameter *np = NULL;
417  TAILQ_FOREACH(np, &a->parameter, entries)
418  {
419  if (!np->attribute || !np->value)
420  continue;
421 
422  struct ParameterList pl_conts = TAILQ_HEAD_INITIALIZER(pl_conts);
423  rfc2231_encode_string(&pl_conts, np->attribute, np->value);
424  struct Parameter *cont = NULL;
425  TAILQ_FOREACH(cont, &pl_conts, entries)
426  {
427  fputc(';', fp);
428 
429  buf[0] = 0;
430  mutt_addr_cat(buf, sizeof(buf), cont->value, MimeSpecials);
431 
432  /* Dirty hack to make messages readable by Outlook Express
433  * for the Mac: force quotes around the boundary parameter
434  * even when they aren't needed. */
435  if (!mutt_str_strcasecmp(cont->attribute, "boundary") &&
436  !mutt_str_strcmp(buf, cont->value))
437  snprintf(buf, sizeof(buf), "\"%s\"", cont->value);
438 
439  tmplen = mutt_str_strlen(buf) + mutt_str_strlen(cont->attribute) + 1;
440  if (len + tmplen + 2 > 76)
441  {
442  fputs("\n\t", fp);
443  len = tmplen + 1;
444  }
445  else
446  {
447  fputc(' ', fp);
448  len += tmplen + 1;
449  }
450 
451  fprintf(fp, "%s=%s", cont->attribute, buf);
452  }
453 
454  mutt_param_free(&pl_conts);
455  }
456  }
457 
458  fputc('\n', fp);
459 
460  if (a->language)
461  fprintf(fp, "Content-Language: %s\n", a->language);
462 
463  if (a->description)
464  fprintf(fp, "Content-Description: %s\n", a->description);
465 
466  if (a->disposition != DISP_NONE)
467  {
468  const char *dispstr[] = { "inline", "attachment", "form-data" };
469 
470  if (a->disposition < sizeof(dispstr) / sizeof(char *))
471  {
472  fprintf(fp, "Content-Disposition: %s", dispstr[a->disposition]);
473  len = 21 + mutt_str_strlen(dispstr[a->disposition]);
474 
475  if (a->use_disp && (a->disposition != DISP_INLINE))
476  {
477  char *fn = a->d_filename;
478  if (!fn)
479  fn = a->filename;
480 
481  if (fn)
482  {
483  /* Strip off the leading path... */
484  char *t = strrchr(fn, '/');
485  if (t)
486  t++;
487  else
488  t = fn;
489 
490  struct ParameterList pl_conts = TAILQ_HEAD_INITIALIZER(pl_conts);
491  rfc2231_encode_string(&pl_conts, "filename", t);
492  struct Parameter *cont = NULL;
493  TAILQ_FOREACH(cont, &pl_conts, entries)
494  {
495  fputc(';', fp);
496  buf[0] = 0;
497  mutt_addr_cat(buf, sizeof(buf), cont->value, MimeSpecials);
498 
499  tmplen = mutt_str_strlen(buf) + mutt_str_strlen(cont->attribute) + 1;
500  if (len + tmplen + 2 > 76)
501  {
502  fputs("\n\t", fp);
503  len = tmplen + 1;
504  }
505  else
506  {
507  fputc(' ', fp);
508  len += tmplen + 1;
509  }
510 
511  fprintf(fp, "%s=%s", cont->attribute, buf);
512  }
513 
514  mutt_param_free(&pl_conts);
515  }
516  }
517 
518  fputc('\n', fp);
519  }
520  else
521  {
522  mutt_debug(LL_DEBUG1, "ERROR: invalid content-disposition %d\n", a->disposition);
523  }
524  }
525 
526  if (a->encoding != ENC_7BIT)
527  fprintf(fp, "Content-Transfer-Encoding: %s\n", ENCODING(a->encoding));
528 
529  if ((C_CryptProtectedHeadersWrite
530 #ifdef USE_AUTOCRYPT
531  || C_Autocrypt
532 #endif
533  ) &&
534  a->mime_headers)
535  {
536  mutt_rfc822_write_header(fp, a->mime_headers, NULL, MUTT_WRITE_HEADER_MIME, false, false);
537  }
538 
539  /* Do NOT add the terminator here!!! */
540  return ferror(fp) ? -1 : 0;
541 }

563  FILE *fp_in = NULL;

567  {
568  /* First, find the boundary to use */
569  const char *p = mutt_param_get(&a->parameter, "boundary");
570  if (!p)
571  {
572  mutt_debug(LL_DEBUG1, "no boundary parameter found\n");
573  mutt_error(_("No boundary parameter found [report this error]"));
574  return -1;
575  }
576  char boundary[128];
577  mutt_str_strfcpy(boundary, p, sizeof(boundary));
578 
579  for (struct Body *t = a->parts; t; t = t->next)
580  {
581  fprintf(fp, "\n--%s\n", boundary);
582  if (mutt_write_mime_header(t, fp) == -1)
583  return -1;
584  fputc('\n', fp);
585  if (mutt_write_mime_body(t, fp) == -1)
586  return -1;
587  }
588  fprintf(fp, "\n--%s--\n", boundary);
589  return ferror(fp) ? -1 : 0;
590  }
591 
592  /* This is pretty gross, but it's the best solution for now... */
593  if (((WithCrypto & APPLICATION_PGP) != 0) && (a->type == TYPE_APPLICATION) &&
594  (mutt_str_strcmp(a->subtype, "pgp-encrypted") == 0) && !a->filename)
595  {
596  fputs("Version: 1\n", fp);
597  return 0;
598  }
599 
600  fp_in = fopen(a->filename, "r");
601  if (!fp_in)
602  {
603  mutt_debug(LL_DEBUG1, "%s no longer exists\n", a->filename);
604  mutt_error(_("%s no longer exists"), a->filename);
605  return -1;
606  }
607 
608  if ((a->type == TYPE_TEXT) && (!a->noconv))
609  {
610  char send_charset[128];
611  fc = mutt_ch_fgetconv_open(
612  fp_in, a->charset,
613  mutt_body_get_charset(a, send_charset, sizeof(send_charset)), 0);
614  }
615  else
616  fc = mutt_ch_fgetconv_open(fp_in, 0, 0, 0);
617 
618  mutt_sig_allow_interrupt(true);
619  if (a->encoding == ENC_QUOTED_PRINTABLE)
620  encode_quoted(fc, fp, write_as_text_part(a));
621  else if (a->encoding == ENC_BASE64)
622  encode_base64(fc, fp, write_as_text_part(a));
623  else if ((a->type == TYPE_TEXT) && (!a->noconv))
624  encode_8bit(fc, fp);
625  else
626  mutt_file_copy_stream(fp_in, fp);
627  mutt_sig_allow_interrupt(false);
628 
629  mutt_ch_fgetconv_close(&fc);
630  mutt_file_fclose(&fp_in);
631 
632  if (SigInt == 1)
633  {
634  SigInt = 0;
635  return -1;
636  }
637  return ferror(fp) ? -1 : 0;
638 }

663  bool from = s->from;

668 
669  if (!buf) /* This signals EOF */
670  {
671  if (was_cr)
672  info->binary = true;
673  if (linelen > info->linemax)
674  info->linemax = linelen;
675 
676  return;
677  }
678 
679  for (; buflen; buf++, buflen--)
680  {
681  char ch = *buf;
682 
683  if (was_cr)
684  {
685  was_cr = false;
686  if (ch == '\n')
687  {
688  if (whitespace)
689  info->space = true;
690  if (dot)
691  info->dot = true;
692  if (linelen > info->linemax)
693  info->linemax = linelen;
694  whitespace = 0;
695  dot = false;
696  linelen = 0;
697  continue;
698  }
699 
700  info->binary = true;
701  }
702 
703  linelen++;
704  if (ch == '\n')
705  {
706  info->crlf++;
707  if (whitespace)
708  info->space = true;
709  if (dot)
710  info->dot = true;
711  if (linelen > info->linemax)
712  info->linemax = linelen;
713  whitespace = 0;
714  linelen = 0;
715  dot = false;
716  }
717  else if (ch == '\r')
718  {
719  info->crlf++;
720  info->cr = true;
721  was_cr = true;
722  continue;
723  }
724  else if (ch & 0x80)
725  info->hibin++;
726  else if ((ch == '\t') || (ch == '\f'))
727  {
728  info->ascii++;
729  whitespace++;
730  }
731  else if (ch == 0)
732  {
733  info->nulbin++;
734  info->lobin++;
735  }
736  else if ((ch < 32) || (ch == 127))
737  info->lobin++;
738  else
739  {
740  if (linelen == 1)
741  {
742  if ((ch == 'F') || (ch == 'f'))
743  from = true;
744  else
745  from = false;
746  if (ch == '.')
747  dot = true;
748  else
749  dot = false;
750  }
751  else if (from)
752  {
753  if ((linelen == 2) && (ch != 'r'))
754  from = false;
755  else if ((linelen == 3) && (ch != 'o'))
756  from = false;
757  else if (linelen == 4)
758  {
759  if (ch == 'm')
760  info->from = true;
761  from = false;
762  }
763  }
764  if (ch == ' ')
765  whitespace++;
766  info->ascii++;
767  }
768 
769  if (linelen > 1)
770  dot = false;
771  if ((ch != ' ') && (ch != '\t'))
772  whitespace = 0;
773  }
774 
775  s->from = from;
776  s->whitespace = whitespace;
777  s->dot = dot;
778  s->linelen = linelen;
779  s->was_cr = was_cr;
780 }

◆ convert_file_to()

static size_t convert_file_to ( FILE *  fp,
const char *  fromcode,
int  ncodes,
char const *const *  tocodes,
int *  tocode,
struct Content *  info 
)
static

Change the encoding of a file.

Parameters
[in]fpFile to convert
[in]fromcodeOriginal encoding
[in]ncodesNumber of target encodings
[in]tocodesList of target encodings
[out]tocodeChosen encoding
[in]infoEncoding information
Return values
-1Error, no conversion was possible
>0Success, number of bytes converted

Find the best charset conversion of the file from fromcode into one of the tocodes. If successful, set *tocode and Content *info and return the number of characters converted inexactly.

We convert via UTF-8 in order to avoid the condition -1(EINVAL), which would otherwise prevent us from knowing the number of inexact conversions. Where the candidate target charset is UTF-8 we avoid doing the second conversion because iconv_open("UTF-8", "UTF-8") fails with some libraries.

We assume that the output from iconv is never more than 4 times as long as the input for any pair of charsets we might be interested in.

Definition at line 807 of file sendlib.c.

809 {
810  char bufi[256], bufu[512], bufo[4 * sizeof(bufi)];
811  size_t ret;
812 
813  const iconv_t cd1 = mutt_ch_iconv_open("utf-8", fromcode, 0);
814  if (cd1 == (iconv_t)(-1))
815  return -1;
816 
817  iconv_t *cd = mutt_mem_calloc(ncodes, sizeof(iconv_t));
818  size_t *score = mutt_mem_calloc(ncodes, sizeof(size_t));
819  struct ContentState *states = mutt_mem_calloc(ncodes, sizeof(struct ContentState));
820  struct Content *infos = mutt_mem_calloc(ncodes, sizeof(struct Content));
821 
822  for (int i = 0; i < ncodes; i++)
823  {
824  if (mutt_str_strcasecmp(tocodes[i], "utf-8") != 0)
825  cd[i] = mutt_ch_iconv_open(tocodes[i], "utf-8", 0);
826  else
827  {
828  /* Special case for conversion to UTF-8 */
829  cd[i] = (iconv_t)(-1);
830  score[i] = (size_t)(-1);
831  }
832  }
833 
834  rewind(fp);
835  size_t ibl = 0;
836  while (true)
837  {
838  /* Try to fill input buffer */
839  size_t n = fread(bufi + ibl, 1, sizeof(bufi) - ibl, fp);
840  ibl += n;
841 
842  /* Convert to UTF-8 */
843  const char *ib = bufi;
844  char *ob = bufu;
845  size_t obl = sizeof(bufu);
846  n = iconv(cd1, (ICONV_CONST char **) ((ibl != 0) ? &ib : 0), &ibl, &ob, &obl);
847  /* assert(n == (size_t)(-1) || !n); */
848  if ((n == (size_t)(-1)) && (((errno != EINVAL) && (errno != E2BIG)) || (ib == bufi)))
849  {
850  /* assert(errno == EILSEQ || (errno == EINVAL && ib == bufi && ibl < sizeof(bufi))); */
851  ret = (size_t)(-1);
852  break;
853  }
854  const size_t ubl1 = ob - bufu;
855 
856  /* Convert from UTF-8 */
857  for (int i = 0; i < ncodes; i++)
858  {
859  if ((cd[i] != (iconv_t)(-1)) && (score[i] != (size_t)(-1)))
860  {
861  const char *ub = bufu;
862  size_t ubl = ubl1;
863  ob = bufo;
864  obl = sizeof(bufo);
865  n = iconv(cd[i], (ICONV_CONST char **) ((ibl || ubl) ? &ub : 0), &ubl, &ob, &obl);
866  if (n == (size_t)(-1))
867  {
868  /* assert(errno == E2BIG || (BUGGY_ICONV && (errno == EILSEQ || errno == ENOENT))); */
869  score[i] = (size_t)(-1);
870  }
871  else
872  {
873  score[i] += n;
874  update_content_info(&infos[i], &states[i], bufo, ob - bufo);
875  }
876  }
877  else if ((cd[i] == (iconv_t)(-1)) && (score[i] == (size_t)(-1)))
878  {
879  /* Special case for conversion to UTF-8 */
880  update_content_info(&infos[i], &states[i], bufu, ubl1);
881  }
882  }
883 
884  if (ibl)
885  {
886  /* Save unused input */
887  memmove(bufi, ib, ibl);
888  }
889  else if (!ubl1 && (ib < bufi + sizeof(bufi)))
890  {
891  ret = 0;
892  break;
893  }
894  }
895 
896  if (ret == 0)
897  {
898  /* Find best score */
899  ret = (size_t)(-1);
900  for (int i = 0; i < ncodes; i++)
901  {
902  if ((cd[i] == (iconv_t)(-1)) && (score[i] == (size_t)(-1)))
903  {
904  /* Special case for conversion to UTF-8 */
905  *tocode = i;
906  ret = 0;
907  break;
908  }
909  else if ((cd[i] == (iconv_t)(-1)) || (score[i] == (size_t)(-1)))
910  continue;
911  else if ((ret == (size_t)(-1)) || (score[i] < ret))
912  {
913  *tocode = i;
914  ret = score[i];
915  if (ret == 0)
916  break;
917  }
918  }
919  if (ret != (size_t)(-1))
920  {
921  memcpy(info, &infos[*tocode], sizeof(struct Content));
922  update_content_info(info, &states[*tocode], 0, 0); /* EOF */
923  }
924  }
925 
926  for (int i = 0; i < ncodes; i++)
927  if (cd[i] != (iconv_t)(-1))
928  iconv_close(cd[i]);
929 
930  iconv_close(cd1);
931  FREE(&cd);
932  FREE(&infos);
933  FREE(&score);
934  FREE(&states);
935 
936  return ret;
937 }

◆ convert_file_from_to()

static size_t convert_file_from_to ( FILE *  fp,
const char *  fromcodes,
const char *  tocodes,
char **  fromcode,
char **  tocode,
struct Content *  info 
)
static

Convert a file between encodings.

Parameters
[in]fpFile to read from
[in]fromcodesCharsets to try converting FROM
[in]tocodesCharsets to try converting TO
[out]fromcodeFrom charset selected
[out]tocodeTo charset selected
[out]infoInfo about the file
Return values
numCharacters converted
-1Error (as a size_t)

Find the first of the fromcodes that gives a valid conversion and the best charset conversion of the file into one of the tocodes. If successful, set *fromcode and *tocode to dynamically allocated strings, set Content *info, and return the number of characters converted inexactly. If no conversion was possible, return -1.

Both fromcodes and tocodes may be colon-separated lists of charsets. However, if fromcode is zero then fromcodes is assumed to be the name of a single charset even if it contains a colon.

Definition at line 960 of file sendlib.c.

962 {
963  char *fcode = NULL;
964  char **tcode = NULL;
965  const char *c = NULL, *c1 = NULL;
966  size_t ret;
967  int ncodes, i, cn;
968 
969  /* Count the tocodes */
970  ncodes = 0;
971  for (c = tocodes; c; c = c1 ? c1 + 1 : 0)
972  {
973  c1 = strchr(c, ':');
974  if (c1 == c)
975  continue;
976  ncodes++;
977  }
978 
979  /* Copy them */
980  tcode = mutt_mem_malloc(ncodes * sizeof(char *));
981  for (c = tocodes, i = 0; c; c = c1 ? c1 + 1 : 0, i++)
982  {
983  c1 = strchr(c, ':');
984  if (c1 == c)
985  continue;
986  tcode[i] = mutt_str_substr_dup(c, c1);
987  }
988 
989  ret = (size_t)(-1);
990  if (fromcode)
991  {
992  /* Try each fromcode in turn */
993  for (c = fromcodes; c; c = c1 ? c1 + 1 : 0)
994  {
995  c1 = strchr(c, ':');
996  if (c1 == c)
997  continue;
998  fcode = mutt_str_substr_dup(c, c1);
999 
1000  ret = convert_file_to(fp, fcode, ncodes, (char const *const *) tcode, &cn, info);
1001  if (ret != (size_t)(-1))
1002  {
1003  *fromcode = fcode;
1004  *tocode = tcode[cn];
1005  tcode[cn] = 0;
1006  break;
1007  }
1008  FREE(&fcode);
1009  }
1010  }
1011  else
1012  {
1013  /* There is only one fromcode */
1014  ret = convert_file_to(fp, fromcodes, ncodes, (char const *const *) tcode, &cn, info);
1015  if (ret != (size_t)(-1))
1016  {
1017  *tocode = tcode[cn];
1018  tcode[cn] = 0;
1019  }
1020  }
1021 
1022  /* Free memory */
1023  for (i = 0; i < ncodes; i++)
1024  FREE(&tcode[i]);
1025 
1026  FREE(&tcode);
1027 
1028  return ret;
1029 }

◆ mutt_lookup_mime_type()

enum ContentType mutt_lookup_mime_type ( struct Body *  att,
const char *  path 
)

Find the MIME type for an attachment.

Parameters
attEmail with attachment
pathPath to attachment
Return values
numMIME type, e.g. TYPE_IMAGE

Given a file at 'path', see if there is a registered MIME type. Returns the major MIME type, and copies the subtype to "d". First look in a system mime.types if we can find one, then look for ~/.mime.types. The longest match is used so that we can match 'ps.gz' when 'gz' also exists.

Definition at line 1127 of file sendlib.c.

1128 {
1129  FILE *fp = NULL;
1130  char *p = NULL, *q = NULL, *ct = NULL;
1131  char buf[PATH_MAX];
1132  char subtype[256] = { 0 };
1133  char xtype[256] = { 0 };
1134  int sze, cur_sze = 0;
1135  bool found_mimetypes = false;
1136  enum ContentType type = TYPE_OTHER;
1137 
1138  int szf = mutt_str_strlen(path);
1139 
1140  for (int count = 0; count < 4; count++)
1141  {
1142  /* can't use strtok() because we use it in an inner loop below, so use
1143  * a switch statement here instead. */
1144  switch (count)
1145  {
1146  /* last file with last entry to match wins type/xtype */
1147  case 0:
1148  /* check default unix mimetypes location first */
1149  mutt_str_strfcpy(buf, "/etc/mime.types", sizeof(buf));
1150  break;
1151  case 1:
1152  mutt_str_strfcpy(buf, SYSCONFDIR "/mime.types", sizeof(buf));
1153  break;
1154  case 2:
1155  mutt_str_strfcpy(buf, PKGDATADIR "/mime.types", sizeof(buf));
1156  break;
1157  case 3:
1158  snprintf(buf, sizeof(buf), "%s/.mime.types", NONULL(HomeDir));
1159  break;
1160  default:
1161  mutt_debug(LL_DEBUG1, "Internal error, count = %d\n", count);
1162  goto bye; /* shouldn't happen */
1163  }
1164 
1165  fp = fopen(buf, "r");
1166  if (fp)
1167  {
1168  found_mimetypes = true;
1169 
1170  while (fgets(buf, sizeof(buf) - 1, fp))
1171  {
1172  /* weed out any comments */
1173  p = strchr(buf, '#');
1174  if (p)
1175  *p = '\0';
1176 
1177  /* remove any leading space. */
1178  ct = buf;
1179  SKIPWS(ct);
1180 
1181  /* position on the next field in this line */
1182  p = strpbrk(ct, " \t");
1183  if (!p)
1184  continue;
1185  *p++ = 0;
1186  SKIPWS(p);
1187 
1188  /* cycle through the file extensions */
1189  while ((p = strtok(p, " \t\n")))
1190  {
1191  sze = mutt_str_strlen(p);
1192  if ((sze > cur_sze) && (szf >= sze) &&
1193  (mutt_str_strcasecmp(path + szf - sze, p) == 0) &&
1194  ((szf == sze) || (path[szf - sze - 1] == '.')))
1195  {
1196  /* get the content-type */
1197 
1198  p = strchr(ct, '/');
1199  if (!p)
1200  {
1201  /* malformed line, just skip it. */
1202  break;
1203  }
1204  *p++ = 0;
1205 
1206  for (q = p; *q && !IS_SPACE(*q); q++)
1207  ; // do nothing
1208 
1209  mutt_str_substr_copy(p, q, subtype, sizeof(subtype));
1210 
1211  type = mutt_check_mime_type(ct);
1212  if (type == TYPE_OTHER)
1213  mutt_str_strfcpy(xtype, ct, sizeof(xtype));
1214 
1215  cur_sze = sze;
1216  }
1217  p = NULL;
1218  }
1219  }
1220  mutt_file_fclose(&fp);
1221  }
1222  }
1223 
1224 bye:
1225 
1226  /* no mime.types file found */
1227  if (!found_mimetypes)
1228  {
1229  mutt_error(_("Could not find any mime.types file."));
1230  }
1231 
1232  if ((type != TYPE_OTHER) || (*xtype != '\0'))
1233  {
1234  att->type = type;
1235  mutt_str_replace(&att->subtype, subtype);
1236  mutt_str_replace(&att->xtype, xtype);
1237  }
1238 
1239  return type;
1240 }

)

Parameters
aBody of the email

Definition at line 1247 of file sendlib.c.

1248 {
1249  struct Buffer *buf = NULL;
1250  struct State s = { 0 };
1251  struct stat sb;
1252 
1253  for (; a; a = a->next)
1254  {
1255  if (a->type == TYPE_MULTIPART)
1256  {
1257  a->encoding = ENC_7BIT;
1258  transform_to_7bit(a->parts, fp_in);
1259  }
1260  else if (mutt_is_message_type(a->type, a->subtype))
1261  {
1262  mutt_message_to_7bit(a, fp_in);
1263  }
1264  else
1265  {
1266  a->noconv = true;
1267  a->force_charset = true;
1268 
1269  /* Because of the potential recursion in message types, we
1270  * restrict the lifetime of the buffer tightly */
1271  buf = mutt_buffer_pool_get();
1272  mutt_buffer_mktemp(buf);
1273  s.fp_out = mutt_file_fopen(mutt_b2s(buf), "w");
1274  if (!s.fp_out)
1275  {
1276  mutt_perror("fopen");
1277  mutt_buffer_pool_release(&buf);
1278  return;
1279  }
1280  s.fp_in = fp_in;
1281  mutt_decode_attachment(a, &s);
1282  mutt_file_fclose(&s.fp_out);
1283  FREE(&a->d_filename);
1284  a->d_filename = a->filename;
1285  a->filename = mutt_buffer_strdup(buf);
1286  mutt_buffer_pool_release(&buf);
1287  a->unlink = true;
1288  if (stat(a->filename, &sb) == -1)
1289  {
1290  mutt_perror("stat");
1291  return;
1292  }
1293  a->length = sb.st_size;
1294 
1295  mutt_update_encoding(a);
1296  if (a->encoding == ENC_8BIT)
1297  a->encoding = ENC_QUOTED_PRINTABLE;
1298  else if (a->encoding == ENC_BINARY)
1299  a->encoding = ENC_BASE64;
1300  }
1301  }
1302 }

1313  FILE *fp_out = NULL;
1314  struct stat sb;
1315

1317  fp_in = fp;
1318  else if (!a->filename || !(fp_in = fopen(a->filename, "r")))
1319  {
1320  mutt_error(_("Could not open %s"), a->filename ? a->filename : "(null)");
1321  return;
1322  }
1323  else
1324  {
1325  a->offset = 0;
1326  if (stat(a->filename, &sb) == -1)
1327  {
1328  mutt_perror("stat");
1329  mutt_file_fclose(&fp_in);
1330  goto cleanup;
1331  }
1332  a->length = sb.st_size;
1333  }
1334 
1335  /* Avoid buffer pool due to recursion */
1336  mutt_buffer_mktemp(&temp);
1337  fp_out = mutt_file_fopen(mutt_b2s(&temp), "w+");
1338  if (!fp_out)
1339  {
1340  mutt_perror("fopen");
1341  goto cleanup;
1342  }
1343

1348

1350  CH_MIME | CH_NONEWLINE | CH_XMIT, NULL, 0);

1352  fputs("MIME-Version: 1.0\n", fp_out);
1353  mutt_write_mime_header(a->parts, fp_out);
1354  fputc('\n', fp_out);
1355  mutt_write_mime_body(a->parts, fp_out);
1356

1358  mutt_file_fclose(&fp_in);
1359  mutt_file_fclose(&fp_out);
1360 
1361  a->encoding = ENC_7BIT;
1362  FREE(&a->d_filename);

1365  unlink(a->filename);
1366  a->filename = mutt_buffer_strdup(&temp);
1367  a->unlink = true;
1368  if (stat(a->filename, &sb) == -1)
1369  {
1370  mutt_perror("stat");
1371  goto cleanup;
1372  }
1373  a->length = sb.st_size;
1374  mutt_body_free(&a->parts);

1378  if (fp_in && (fp_in != fp))
1379  mutt_file_fclose(&fp_in);
1380 
1381  if (fp_out)
1382  {
1383  mutt_file_fclose(&fp_out);
1384  mutt_file_unlink(mutt_b2s(&temp));

static

determine which Content-Transfer-Encoding to use

Parameters
[in]bBody of email

1397  if (b->type == TYPE_TEXT)
1398  {
1399  char send_charset[128];
1400  char *chsname = mutt_body_get_charset(b, send_charset, sizeof(send_charset));
1401  if ((info->lobin && !mutt_str_startswith(chsname, "iso-2022", CASE_IGNORE)) ||
1402  (info->linemax > 990) || (info->from && C_EncodeFrom))
1403  {
1404  b->encoding = ENC_QUOTED_PRINTABLE;
1405  }
1406  else if (info->hibin)
1407  {
1408  b->encoding = C_Allow8bit ? ENC_8BIT : ENC_QUOTED_PRINTABLE;
1409  }
1410  else
1411  {
1412  b->encoding = ENC_7BIT;
1413  }
1414  }
1415  else if ((b->type == TYPE_MESSAGE) || (b->type == TYPE_MULTIPART))
1416  {
1417  if (info->lobin || info->hibin)
1418  {
1419  if (C_Allow8bit && !info->lobin)
1420  b->encoding = ENC_8BIT;
1421  else
1422  mutt_message_to_7bit(b, NULL);
1423  }
1424  else
1425  b->encoding = ENC_7BIT;
1426  }
1427  else if ((b->type == TYPE_APPLICATION) &&
1428  (mutt_str_strcasecmp(b->subtype, "pgp-keys") == 0))
1429  {
1430  b->encoding = ENC_7BIT;
1431  }
1432  else
1433  {
1434  /* Determine which encoding is smaller */
1435  if (1.33 * (float) (info->lobin + info->hibin + info->ascii) <
1436  3.0 * (float) (info->lobin + info->hibin) + (float) info->ascii)
1437  {
1438  b->encoding = ENC_BASE64;
1439  }
1440  else
1441  {
1442  b->encoding = ENC_QUOTED_PRINTABLE;
1443  }
1444  }
1445 }

eEmail

Return values

1520 {
1521  struct Body *body = NULL;
1522  FILE *fp = NULL;
1523  CopyMessageFlags cmflags;
1524  SecurityFlags pgp = WithCrypto ? e->security : SEC_NO_FLAGS;
1525 
1526  if (WithCrypto)
1527  {
1528  if ((C_MimeForwardDecode || C_ForwardDecrypt) && (e->security & SEC_ENCRYPT))
1529  {
1530  if (!crypt_valid_passphrase(e->security))
1531  return NULL;
1532  }
1533  }
1534 
1535  struct Buffer *buf = mutt_buffer_pool_get();
1536  mutt_buffer_mktemp(buf);
1537  fp = mutt_file_fopen(mutt_b2s(buf), "w+");
1538  if (!fp)
1539  {
1540  mutt_buffer_pool_release(&buf);
1541  return NULL;
1542  }
1543 
1544  body = mutt_body_new();
1545  body->type = TYPE_MESSAGE;
1546  body->subtype = mutt_str_strdup("rfc822");
1547  body->filename = mutt_str_strdup(mutt_b2s(buf));
1548  body->unlink = true;
1549  body->use_disp = false;
1550  body->disposition = DISP_INLINE;
1551  body->noconv = true;
1552 
1553  mutt_buffer_pool_release(&buf);
1554 
1555  mutt_parse_mime_message(m, e);
1556 
1557  CopyHeaderFlags chflags = CH_XMIT;
1558  cmflags = MUTT_CM_NO_FLAGS;
1559 
1560  /* If we are attaching a message, ignore C_MimeForwardDecode */
1561  if (!attach_msg && C_MimeForwardDecode)
1562  {
1563  chflags |= CH_MIME | CH_TXTPLAIN;
1564  cmflags = MUTT_CM_DECODE | MUTT_CM_CHARCONV;
1565  if (WithCrypto & APPLICATION_PGP)
1566  pgp &= ~PGP_ENCRYPT;
1567  if (WithCrypto & APPLICATION_SMIME)
1568  pgp &= ~SMIME_ENCRYPT;
1569  }
1570  else if ((WithCrypto != 0) && C_ForwardDecrypt && (e->security & SEC_ENCRYPT))
1571  {
1572  if (((WithCrypto & APPLICATION_PGP) != 0) && mutt_is_multipart_encrypted(e->content))
1573  {
1574  chflags |= CH_MIME | CH_NONEWLINE;
1575  cmflags = MUTT_CM_DECODE_PGP;
1576  pgp &= ~PGP_ENCRYPT;
1577  }
1578  else if (((WithCrypto & APPLICATION_PGP) != 0) &&
1579  ((mutt_is_application_pgp(e->content) & PGP_ENCRYPT) == PGP_ENCRYPT))
1580  {
1581  chflags |= CH_MIME | CH_TXTPLAIN;
1582  cmflags = MUTT_CM_DECODE | MUTT_CM_CHARCONV;
1583  pgp &= ~PGP_ENCRYPT;
1584  }
1585  else if (((WithCrypto & APPLICATION_SMIME) != 0) &&
1586  ((mutt_is_application_smime(e->content) & SMIME_ENCRYPT) == SMIME_ENCRYPT))
1587  {
1588  chflags |= CH_MIME | CH_TXTPLAIN;
1589  cmflags = MUTT_CM_DECODE | MUTT_CM_CHARCONV;
1590  pgp &= ~SMIME_ENCRYPT;
1591  }
1592  }
1593 
1594  mutt_copy_message(fp, m, e, cmflags, chflags, 0);
1595 
1596  fflush(fp);
1597  rewind(fp);
1598 
1599  body->email = email_new();
1600  body->email->offset = 0;
1601  /* we don't need the user headers here */
1602  body->email->env = mutt_rfc822_read_header(fp, body->email, false, false);
1603  if (WithCrypto)
1604  body->email->security = pgp;
1605  mutt_update_encoding(body);
1606  body->parts = body->email->content;
1607 
1608  mutt_file_fclose(&fp);
1609 
1610  return body;
1611 }

1625  struct Buffer *cmd = mutt_buffer_pool_get();
1626

1628

1630  if (pid < 0)
1631  {
1632  mutt_error(_("Error running \"%s\""), mutt_b2s(cmd));
1633  mutt_buffer_pool_release(&cmd);
1634  return;
1635  }
1636  mutt_buffer_pool_release(&cmd);
1637 
1638  buf = mutt_file_read_line(buf, &buflen, fp, NULL, 0);

1640  {
1641  if (strchr(buf, '/'))

1643  FREE(&buf);

Create a file attachment.

Parameters
pathFile to attach
Return values
ptrNewly allocated Body
NULLError

1658 {
1659  struct Body *att = mutt_body_new();
1660  att->filename = mutt_str_strdup(path);
1661 
1662  if (C_MimeTypeQueryCommand && C_MimeTypeQueryFirst)
1663  run_mime_type_query(att);
1664 
1665  /* Attempt to determine the appropriate content-type based on the filename
1666  * suffix. */
1667  if (!att->subtype)
1668  mutt_lookup_mime_type(att, path);
1669 
1670  if (!att->subtype && C_MimeTypeQueryCommand && !C_MimeTypeQueryFirst)
1671  {
1672  run_mime_type_query(att);
1673  }
1674 
1675  struct Content *info = mutt_get_content_info(path, att);
1676  if (!info)
1677  {
1678  mutt_body_free(&att);
1679  return NULL;
1680  }
1681 
1682  if (!att->subtype)
1683  {
1684  if ((info->nulbin == 0) &&
1685  ((info->lobin == 0) || ((info->lobin + info->hibin + info->ascii) / info->lobin >= 10)))
1686  {
1687  /* Statistically speaking, there should be more than 10% "lobin"
1688  * chars if this is really a binary file... */
1689  att->type = TYPE_TEXT;
1690  att->subtype = mutt_str_strdup("plain");
1691  }
1692  else
1693  {
1694  att->type = TYPE_APPLICATION;
1695  att->subtype = mutt_str_strdup("octet-stream");
1696  }
1697  }
1698 
1699  FREE(&info);
1700  mutt_update_encoding(att);
1701  return att;
1702 }

bBody of email to start
Return values
ptrNewly allocated Body

1754 {
1755  struct Body *new_body = mutt_body_new();
1756  new_body->type = TYPE_MULTIPART;
1757  new_body->subtype = mutt_str_strdup("mixed");
1758  new_body->encoding = get_toplevel_encoding(b);
1759  do
1760  {
1761  mutt_generate_boundary(&new_body->parameter);
1762  if (check_boundary(mutt_param_get(&new_body->parameter, "boundary"), b))
1763  mutt_param_delete(&new_body->parameter, "boundary");
1764  } while (!mutt_param_get(&new_body->parameter, "boundary"));
1765  new_body->use_disp = false;
1766  new_body->disposition = DISP_INLINE;
1767  new_body->parts = b;
1768 
1769  return new_body;
1770 }

1804 {
1805  char buf[1024];
1806  int count = 0;
1807 
1808  struct Address *a = NULL;
1809  TAILQ_FOREACH(a, al, entries)
1810  {
1811  buf[0] = '\0';
1812  mutt_addr_write(buf, sizeof(buf), a, display);
1813  size_t len = mutt_str_strlen(buf);
1814  if (count && (linelen + len > 74))
1815  {
1816  fputs("\n\t", fp);
1817  linelen = len + 8; /* tab is usually about 8 spaces... */
1818  }
1819  else
1820  {
1821  if (count && a->mailbox)
1822  {
1823  fputc(' ', fp);
1824  linelen++;
1825  }
1826  linelen += len;
1827  }
1828  fputs(buf, fp);
1829  struct Address *next = TAILQ_NEXT(a, entries);
1830  if (!a->group && next && next->mailbox)
1831  {
1832  linelen++;
1833  fputc(',', fp);
1834  }
1835  count++;
1836  }
1837  fputc('\n', fp);
1838 }

◆ mutt_write_references()

void mutt_write_references ( const struct ListHead *  r,
FILE *  fp,
size_t  trim 
)

Add the message references to a list.

Parameters
rString List of references
fpFile to write to
trimTrim the list to at most this many items

Write the list in reverse because they are stored in reverse order when parsed to speed up threading.

Definition at line 1849 of file sendlib.c.

1850 {
1851  struct ListNode *np = NULL;
1852  size_t length = 0;
1853 
1854  STAILQ_FOREACH(np, r, entries)
1855  {
1856  if (++length == trim)
1857  break;
1858  }
1859 
1860  struct ListNode **ref = mutt_mem_calloc(length, sizeof(struct ListNode *));
1861 
1862  // store in reverse order
1863  size_t tmp = length;
1864  STAILQ_FOREACH(np, r, entries)
1865  {
1866  ref[--tmp] = np;
1867  if (tmp == 0)
1868  break;
1869  }
1870 
1871  for (size_t i = 0; i < length; i++)
1872  {
1873  fputc(' ', fp);
1874  fputs(ref[i]->data, fp);
1875  if (i != length - 1)
1876  fputc('\n', fp);
1877  }
1878 
1879  FREE(&ref);
1880 }

◆ print_val()

static int print_val ( FILE *  fp,
const char *  pfx,
const char *  value,
CopyHeaderFlags  chflags,
size_t  col 
)
static

Add pieces to an email header, wrapping where necessary.

Parameters
fpFile to write to
pfxPrefix for headers
valueText to be added
chflagsFlags, see CopyHeaderFlags
colColumn that this text starts at
Return values
0Success
-1Failure

Definition at line 1892 of file sendlib.c.

1894 {
1895  while (value && (value[0] != '\0'))
1896  {
1897  if (fputc(*value, fp) == EOF)
1898  return -1;
1899  /* corner-case: break words longer than 998 chars by force,
1900  * mandated by RFC5322 */
1901  if (!(chflags & CH_DISPLAY) && (++col >= 998))
1902  {
1903  if (fputs("\n ", fp) < 0)
1904  return -1;
1905  col = 1;
1906  }
1907  if (*value == '\n')
1908  {
1909  if ((value[1] != '\0') && pfx && (pfx[0] != '\0') && (fputs(pfx, fp) == EOF))
1910  return -1;
1911  /* for display, turn folding spaces into folding tabs */
1912  if ((chflags & CH_DISPLAY) && ((value[1] == ' ') || (value[1] == '\t')))
1913  {
1914  value++;

1918  return -1;
1919  continue;

1921  }

1924  return 0;

static int fold_one_header ( FILE *  fp,
const char *  tag,
const char *  value,
size_t  vlen,
const char *  pfx,
int  wraplen,
CopyHeaderFlags  chflags 
)
static

Fold one header line.

Parameters
fpFile to write to
tagHeader key, e.g. "From"
valueHeader value
vlenLength of the header value string
pfxPrefix for header
wraplenColumn to wrap at
chflagsFlags, see CopyHeaderFlags
Return values
0Success
-1Failure

Definition at line 1939 of file sendlib.c.

1941 {
1942  if (!value || (*value == '\0') || !vlen)
1943  return 0;
1944 
1945  const char *p = value;
1946  char buf[8192] = { 0 };
1947  int first = 1, col = 0, l = 0;
1948  const bool display = (chflags & CH_DISPLAY);
1949 
1950  mutt_debug(LL_DEBUG5, "pfx=[%s], tag=[%s], flags=%d value=[%.*s]\n", pfx, tag,
1951  chflags, ((value[vlen - 1] == '\n') ? vlen - 1 : vlen), value);
1952 
1953  if (tag && *tag && (fprintf(fp, "%s%s: ", NONULL(pfx), tag) < 0))
1954  return -1;
1955  col = mutt_str_strlen(tag) + ((tag && (tag[0] != '\0')) ? 2 : 0) + mutt_str_strlen(pfx);
1956 
1957  while (p && (p[0] != '\0'))
1958  {
1959  int fold = 0;
1960 
1961  /* find the next word and place it in 'buf'. it may start with
1962  * whitespace we can fold before */
1963  const char *next = mutt_str_find_word(p);
1964  l = MIN(sizeof(buf) - 1, next - p);
1965  memcpy(buf, p, l);
1966  buf[l] = '\0';
1967 
1968  /* determine width: character cells for display, bytes for sending
1969  * (we get pure ascii only) */
1970  const int w = mutt_mb_width(buf, col, display);
1971  const int enc = mutt_str_startswith(buf, "=?", CASE_MATCH);
1972 
1973  mutt_debug(LL_DEBUG5, "word=[%s], col=%d, w=%d, next=[0x0%x]\n",
1974  (buf[0] == '\n' ? "\\n" : buf), col, w, *next);
1975 
1976  /* insert a folding \n before the current word's lwsp except for
1977  * header name, first word on a line (word longer than wrap width)
1978  * and encoded words */
1979  if (!first && !enc && col && ((col + w) >= wraplen))
1980  {
1981  col = mutt_str_strlen(pfx);
1982  fold = 1;
1983  if (fprintf(fp, "\n%s", NONULL(pfx)) <= 0)
1984  return -1;
1985  }
1986 
1987  /* print the actual word; for display, ignore leading ws for word
1988  * and fold with tab for readability */
1989  if (display && fold)
1990  {
1991  char *pc = buf;
1992  while ((pc[0] != '\0') && ((pc[0] == ' ') || (pc[0] == '\t')))
1993  {
1994  pc++;
1995  col--;
1996  }
1997  if (fputc('\t', fp) == EOF)
1998  return -1;
1999  if (print_val(fp, pfx, pc, chflags, col) < 0)
2000  return -1;
2001  col += 8;
2002  }
2003  else if (print_val(fp, pfx, buf, chflags, col) < 0)
2004  return -1;
2005  col += w;
2006 
2007  /* if the current word ends in \n, ignore all its trailing spaces
2008  * and reset column; this prevents us from putting only spaces (or
2009  * even none) on a line if the trailing spaces are located at our
2010  * current line width
2011  * XXX this covers ASCII space only, for display we probably
2012  * want something like iswspace() here */
2013  const char *sp = next;
2014  while ((sp[0] != '\0') && ((sp[0] == ' ') || (sp[0] == '\t')))
2015  sp++;
2016  if (sp[0] == '\n')
2017  {
2018  if (sp[1] == '\0')
2019  break;
2020  next = sp;
2021  col = 0;
2022  }
2023 
2024  p = next;
2025  first = 0;
2026  }
2027 
2028  /* if we have printed something but didn't \n-terminate it, do it
2029  * except the last word we printed ended in \n already */
2030  if (col && ((l == 0) || (buf[l - 1] != '\n')))
2031  if (putc('\n', fp) == EOF)
2032  return -1;
2033 
2034  return 0;
2035 }

◆ write_one_header()

static int write_one_header ( FILE *  fp,
int  pfxw,
int  max,
int  wraplen,
const char *  pfx,
const char *  start,
const char *  end,
CopyHeaderFlags  chflags 
)
static

Write out one header line.

Parameters
fpFile to write to
pfxwWidth of prefix string
maxMax width
wraplenColumn to wrap at
pfxPrefix for header
startStart of header line
endEnd of header line
chflagsFlags, see CopyHeaderFlags
Return values
0Success
-1Failure

Definition at line 2086 of file sendlib.c.

2088 {
2089  const char *t = strchr(start, ':');
2090  if (!t || (t > end))
2091  {
2092  mutt_debug(LL_DEBUG1, "#2 warning: header not in 'key: value' format!\n");
2093  return 0;
2094  }
2095 
2096  const size_t vallen = end - start;
2097  const bool short_enough = (pfxw + max <= wraplen);
2098 
2099  mutt_debug((short_enough ? LL_DEBUG2 : LL_DEBUG5), "buf[%s%.*s] %s, max width = %d %s %d\n",
2100  NONULL(pfx), vallen - 1 /* skip newline */, start,
2101  (short_enough ? "short enough" : "too long"), max,
2102  (short_enough ? "<=" : ">"), wraplen);
2103 
2104  int rc = 0;
2105  const char *valbuf = NULL, *tagbuf = NULL;
2106  const bool is_from = (vallen > 5) && mutt_str_startswith(start, "from ", CASE_IGNORE);
2107 
2108  /* only pass through folding machinery if necessary for sending,
2109  * never wrap From_ headers on sending */
2110  if (!(chflags & CH_DISPLAY) && (short_enough || is_from))
2111  {
2112  if (pfx && *pfx)
2113  {
2114  if (fputs(pfx, fp) == EOF)
2115  {
2116  return -1;
2117  }
2118  }
2119 
2120  valbuf = mutt_str_substr_dup(start, end);
2121  rc = print_val(fp, pfx, valbuf, chflags, mutt_str_strlen(pfx));
2122  }
2123  else
2124  {
2125  if (!is_from)
2126  {
2127  tagbuf = mutt_str_substr_dup(start, t);
2128  /* skip over the colon separating the header field name and value */
2129  t++;
2130 
2131  /* skip over any leading whitespace (WSP, as defined in RFC5322)
2132  * NOTE: mutt_str_skip_email_wsp() does the wrong thing here.
2133  * See tickets 3609 and 3716. */
2134  while ((*t == ' ') || (*t == '\t'))
2135  t++;
2136  }
2137  valbuf = mutt_str_substr_dup(is_from ? start : t, end);
2138  rc = fold_one_header(fp, tagbuf, valbuf, end - (is_from ? start : t), pfx,
2139  wraplen, chflags);
2140  }
2141 
2142  FREE(&tagbuf);
2143  FREE(&valbuf);
2144  return rc;
2145 }

2274  {
2275  continue;
2276  }
2277

2280  {
2281  continue; /* don't emit empty fields. */
2282  }
2283

2287  mutt_array_size(userhdrs_override_headers),
2288  sizeof(char *), userhdrs_override_cmp);

2304

◆ mutt_rfc822_write_header()

int mutt_rfc822_write_header ( FILE *  fp,
struct Envelope *  env,
struct Body *  attach,
enum MuttWriteHeaderMode  mode,
bool  privacy,
bool  hide_protected_subject 
)

Write out one RFC822 header line.

Parameters
fpFile to write to
envEnvelope of email
attachAttachment
modeMode, see MuttWriteHeaderMode
privacyIf true, remove headers that might identify the user
hide_protected_subjectIf true, replace subject header
Return values
0Success
-1Failure
Note
All RFC2047 encoding should be done outside of this routine, except for the "real name." This will allow this routine to be used more than once, if necessary.

Likewise, all IDN processing should happen outside of this routine.

privacy true => will omit any headers which may identify the user. Output generated is suitable for being sent through anonymous remailer chains.

hide_protected_subject: replaces the Subject header with $crypt_protected_headers_subject in NORMAL or POSTPONE mode.

Definition at line 2332 of file sendlib.c.

2335 {
2336  char buf[1024];
2337 
2338  if (((mode == MUTT_WRITE_HEADER_NORMAL) || (mode == MUTT_WRITE_HEADER_FCC)) && !privacy)
2339  fputs(mutt_date_make_date(buf, sizeof(buf)), fp);
2340 
2341  /* UseFrom is not consulted here so that we can still write a From:
2342  * field if the user sets it with the 'my_hdr' command */
2343  if (!TAILQ_EMPTY(&env->from) && !privacy)
2344  {
2345  buf[0] = '\0';
2346  mutt_addrlist_write(&env->from, buf, sizeof(buf), false);
2347  fprintf(fp, "From: %s\n", buf);
2348  }
2349 
2350  if (!TAILQ_EMPTY(&env->sender) && !privacy)
2351  {
2352  buf[0] = '\0';
2353  mutt_addrlist_write(&env->sender, buf, sizeof(buf), false);
2354  fprintf(fp, "Sender: %s\n", buf);
2355  }
2356 
2357  if (!TAILQ_EMPTY(&env->to))
2358  {
2359  fputs("To: ", fp);
2360  mutt_write_addrlist(&env->to, fp, 4, 0);
2361  }
2362  else if (mode == MUTT_WRITE_HEADER_EDITHDRS)
2363 #ifdef USE_NNTP
2364  if (!OptNewsSend)
2365 #endif
2366  fputs("To:\n", fp);
2367 
2368  if (!TAILQ_EMPTY(&env->cc))
2369  {
2370  fputs("Cc: ", fp);
2371  mutt_write_addrlist(&env->cc, fp, 4, 0);
2372  }
2373  else if (mode == MUTT_WRITE_HEADER_EDITHDRS)
2374 #ifdef USE_NNTP

2376 #endif
2377  fputs("Cc:\n", fp);
2378 
2379  if (!TAILQ_EMPTY(&env->bcc))
2380  {
2381  if ((mode == MUTT_WRITE_HEADER_POSTPONE) ||
2382  (mode == MUTT_WRITE_HEADER_EDITHDRS) || (mode == MUTT_WRITE_HEADER_FCC) ||
2383  ((mode == MUTT_WRITE_HEADER_NORMAL) && C_WriteBcc))
2384  {
2385  fputs("Bcc: ", fp);
2386  mutt_write_addrlist(&env->bcc, fp, 5, 0);
2387  }
2388  }
2389  else if (mode == MUTT_WRITE_HEADER_EDITHDRS)
2390 #ifdef USE_NNTP
2391  if (!OptNewsSend)
2392 #endif
2393  fputs("Bcc:\n", fp);
2394 
2395 #ifdef USE_NNTP
2396  if (env->newsgroups)
2397  fprintf(fp, "Newsgroups: %s\n", env->newsgroups);
2398  else if ((mode == MUTT_WRITE_HEADER_EDITHDRS) && OptNewsSend)
2399  fputs("Newsgroups:\n", fp);
2400 
2401  if (env->followup_to)
2402  fprintf(fp, "Followup-To: %s\n", env->followup_to);
2403  else if ((mode == MUTT_WRITE_HEADER_EDITHDRS) && OptNewsSend)
2404  fputs("Followup-To:\n", fp);
2405 
2406  if (env->x_comment_to)
2407  fprintf(fp, "X-Comment-To: %s\n", env->x_comment_to);
2408  else if ((mode == MUTT_WRITE_HEADER_EDITHDRS) && OptNewsSend && C_XCommentTo)
2409  fputs("X-Comment-To:\n", fp);
2410 #endif
2411 
2412  if (env->subject)
2413  {
2414  if (hide_protected_subject &&
2415  ((mode == MUTT_WRITE_HEADER_NORMAL) || (mode == MUTT_WRITE_HEADER_FCC) ||
2416  (mode == MUTT_WRITE_HEADER_POSTPONE)))
2417  mutt_write_one_header(fp, "Subject", C_CryptProtectedHeadersSubject, NULL, 0, CH_NO_FLAGS);
2418  else
2419  mutt_write_one_header(fp, "Subject", env->subject, NULL, 0, CH_NO_FLAGS);
2420  }
2421  else if (mode == MUTT_WRITE_HEADER_EDITHDRS)
2422  fputs("Subject:\n", fp);
2423 
2424  /* save message id if the user has set it */
2425  if (env->message_id && !privacy)
2426  fprintf(fp, "Message-ID: %s\n", env->message_id);
2427 
2428  if (!TAILQ_EMPTY(&env->reply_to))
2429  {
2430  fputs("Reply-To: ", fp);
2431  mutt_write_addrlist(&env->reply_to, fp, 10, 0);
2432  }
2433  else if (mode == MUTT_WRITE_HEADER_EDITHDRS)
2434  fputs("Reply-To:\n", fp);
2435 
2436  if (!TAILQ_EMPTY(&env->mail_followup_to))
2437  {
2438 #ifdef USE_NNTP
2439  if (!OptNewsSend)
2440 #endif
2441  {
2442  fputs("Mail-Followup-To: ", fp);
2443  mutt_write_addrlist(&env->mail_followup_to, fp, 18, 0);
2444  }
2445  }
2446 
2447  /* Add any user defined headers */
2448  struct UserHdrsOverride userhdrs_overrides = write_userhdrs(fp, &env->userhdrs, privacy);
2449 
2450  if ((mode == MUTT_WRITE_HEADER_NORMAL) || (mode == MUTT_WRITE_HEADER_FCC) ||
2451  (mode == MUTT_WRITE_HEADER_POSTPONE))
2452  {
2453  if (!STAILQ_EMPTY(&env->references))
2454  {
2455  fputs("References:", fp);
2456  mutt_write_references(&env->references, fp, 10);
2457  fputc('\n', fp);
2458  }
2459 
2460  /* Add the MIME headers */
2461  if (!userhdrs_overrides.is_overridden[USERHDRS_OVERRIDE_CONTENT_TYPE])
2462  {
2463  fputs("MIME-Version: 1.0\n", fp);
2464  mutt_write_mime_header(attach, fp);
2465  }
2466  }
2467 
2468  if (!STAILQ_EMPTY(&env->in_reply_to))
2469  {
2470  fputs("In-Reply-To:", fp);
2471  mutt_write_references(&env->in_reply_to, fp, 0);
2472  fputc('\n', fp);
2473  }
2474 
2475 #ifdef USE_AUTOCRYPT
2476  if (C_Autocrypt)
2477  {
2478  if (mode == MUTT_WRITE_HEADER_NORMAL || mode == MUTT_WRITE_HEADER_FCC)
2479  mutt_autocrypt_write_autocrypt_header(env, fp);
2480  if (mode == MUTT_WRITE_HEADER_MIME)
2481  mutt_autocrypt_write_gossip_headers(env, fp);
2482  }
2483 #endif
2484 
2485  if (((mode == MUTT_WRITE_HEADER_NORMAL) || (mode == MUTT_WRITE_HEADER_FCC)) && !privacy &&
2486  C_UserAgent && !userhdrs_overrides.is_overridden[USERHDRS_OVERRIDE_USER_AGENT])
2487  {
2488  /* Add a vanity header */
2489  fprintf(fp, "User-Agent: NeoMutt/%s%s\n", PACKAGE_VERSION, GitVer);
2490  }
2491 
2492  return (ferror(fp) == 0) ? 0 : -1;
2493 }

static

RFC2047-encode a list of headers.

Parameters
hString List of headers

The strings are encoded in-place.

2515  p = mutt_str_skip_email_wsp(p + 1);

◆ send_msg()

static int send_msg ( const char *  path,
char **  args,
const char *  msg,
char **  tempfile 
)
static

invoke sendmail in a subshell

Parameters
[in]pathPath to program to execute
[in]argsArguments to pass to program
[in]msgTemp file containing message to send
[out]tempfileIf sendmail is put in the background, this points to the temporary file containing the stdout of the child process. If it is NULL, stderr and stdout are not redirected.
Return values
0Success
>0Failure, return code from sendmail

Definition at line 2603 of file sendlib.c.

2604 {
2605  sigset_t set;
2606  int st;
2607 
2608  mutt_sig_block_system();
2609 
2610  sigemptyset(&set);
2611  /* we also don't want to be stopped right now */
2612  sigaddset(&set, SIGTSTP);
2613  sigprocmask(SIG_BLOCK, &set, NULL);
2614 
2615  if ((C_SendmailWait >= 0) && tempfile)
2616  {
2617  struct Buffer *tmp = mutt_buffer_pool_get();
2618  mutt_buffer_mktemp(tmp);
2619  *tempfile = mutt_buffer_strdup(tmp);
2620  mutt_buffer_pool_release(&tmp);
2621  }
2622 
2623  pid_t pid = fork();
2624  if (pid == 0)
2625  {
2626  struct sigaction act, oldalrm;
2627 
2628  /* save parent's ID before setsid() */
2629  pid_t ppid = getppid();
2630 
2631  /* we want the delivery to continue even after the main process dies,
2632  * so we put ourselves into another session right away */
2633  setsid();
2634 
2635  /* next we close all open files */
2636  close(0);
2637 #ifdef OPEN_MAX
2638  for (int fd = tempfile ? 1 : 3; fd < OPEN_MAX; fd++)
2639  close(fd);
2640 #elif defined(_POSIX_OPEN_MAX)
2641  for (int fd = tempfile ? 1 : 3; fd < _POSIX_OPEN_MAX; fd++)
2642  close(fd);
2643 #else
2644  if (tempfile)
2645  {
2646  close(1);
2647  close(2);
2648  }
2649 #endif
2650 
2651  /* now the second fork() */
2652  pid = fork();
2653  if (pid == 0)
2654  {
2655  /* "msg" will be opened as stdin */
2656  if (open(msg, O_RDONLY, 0) < 0)
2657  {
2658  unlink(msg);
2659  _exit(S_ERR);
2660  }
2661  unlink(msg);
2662 
2663  if ((C_SendmailWait >= 0) && tempfile && *tempfile)
2664  {
2665  /* *tempfile will be opened as stdout */
2666  if (open(*tempfile, O_WRONLY | O_APPEND | O_CREAT | O_EXCL, 0600) < 0)
2667  _exit(S_ERR);
2668  /* redirect stderr to *tempfile too */
2669  if (dup(1) < 0)
2670  _exit(S_ERR);
2671  }
2672  else if (tempfile)
2673  {
2674  if (open("/dev/null", O_WRONLY | O_APPEND) < 0) /* stdout */
2675  _exit(S_ERR);
2676  if (open("/dev/null", O_RDWR | O_APPEND) < 0) /* stderr */
2677  _exit(S_ERR);
2678  }
2679 
2680  /* execvpe is a glibc extension */
2681  /* execvpe (path, args, mutt_envlist_getlist()); */
2682  execvp(path, args);
2683  _exit(S_ERR);
2684  }
2685  else if (pid == -1)
2686  {
2687  unlink(msg);
2688  FREE(tempfile);
2689  _exit(S_ERR);
2690  }
2691 
2692  /* C_SendmailWait > 0: interrupt waitpid() after C_SendmailWait seconds
2693  * C_SendmailWait = 0: wait forever
2694  * C_SendmailWait < 0: don't wait */
2695  if (C_SendmailWait > 0)
2696  {
2697  SigAlrm = 0;
2698  act.sa_handler = alarm_handler;
2699 #ifdef SA_INTERRUPT
2700  /* need to make sure waitpid() is interrupted on SIGALRM */
2701  act.sa_flags = SA_INTERRUPT;
2702 #else
2703  act.sa_flags = 0;
2704 #endif
2705  sigemptyset(&act.sa_mask);
2706  sigaction(SIGALRM, &act, &oldalrm);
2707  alarm(C_SendmailWait);
2708  }
2709  else if (C_SendmailWait < 0)
2710  _exit(0xff & EX_OK);
2711 
2712  if (waitpid(pid, &st, 0) > 0)
2713  {
2714  st = WIFEXITED(st) ? WEXITSTATUS(st) : S_ERR;
2715  if (C_SendmailWait && (st == (0xff & EX_OK)) && tempfile && *tempfile)
2716  {
2717  unlink(*tempfile); /* no longer needed */
2718  FREE(tempfile);
2719  }
2720  }
2721  else
2722  {
2723  st = ((C_SendmailWait > 0) && (errno == EINTR) && SigAlrm) ? S_BKG : S_ERR;
2724  if ((C_SendmailWait > 0) && tempfile && *tempfile)
2725  {
2726  unlink(*tempfile);
2727  FREE(tempfile);
2728  }
2729  }
2730 
2731  if (C_SendmailWait > 0)
2732  {
2733  /* reset alarm; not really needed, but... */
2734  alarm(0);
2735  sigaction(SIGALRM, &oldalrm, NULL);
2736  }
2737 
2738  if ((kill(ppid, 0) == -1) && (errno == ESRCH) && tempfile && *tempfile)
2739  {
2740  /* the parent is already dead */
2741  unlink(*tempfile);
2742  FREE(tempfile);
2743  }
2744 
2745  _exit(st);
2746  }
2747 
2748  sigprocmask(SIG_UNBLOCK, &set, NULL);
2749 
2750  if ((pid != -1) && (waitpid(pid, &st, 0) > 0))
2751  st = WIFEXITED(st) ? WEXITSTATUS(st) : S_ERR; /* return child status */
2752  else
2753  st = S_ERR; /* error */
2754 
2755  mutt_sig_unblock_system(true);
2756 
2757  return st;
2758 }

2838  int i;
2839 
2840 #ifdef USE_NNTP
2841  if (OptNewsSend)
2842  {
2843  char cmd[1024];
2844 
2845  mutt_expando_format(cmd, sizeof(cmd), 0, sizeof(cmd), NONULL(C_Inews),
2846  nntp_format_str, 0, MUTT_FORMAT_NO_FLAGS);
2847  if (*cmd == '\0')
2848  {
2849  i = nntp_post(Context->mailbox, msg);
2850  unlink(msg);
2851  return i;
2852  }
2853 
2854  s = mutt_str_strdup(cmd);
2855  }
2856  else
2857 #endif
2858  s = mutt_str_strdup(C_Sendmail);
2859 
2860  /* ensure that $sendmail is set to avoid a crash. http://dev.mutt.org/trac/ticket/3548 */
2861  if (!s)
2862  {
2863  mutt_error(_("$sendmail must be set in order to send mail"));
2864  return -1;
2865  }
2866 
2867  ps = s;
2868  i = 0;
2869  while ((ps = strtok(ps, " ")))
2870  {
2871  if (argslen == argsmax)
2872  mutt_mem_realloc(&args, sizeof(char *) * (argsmax += 5));
2873 
2874  if (i)
2875  {
2876  if (mutt_str_strcmp(ps, "--") == 0)
2877  break;
2878  args[argslen++] = ps;
2879  }
2880  else
2881  {
2882  path = mutt_str_strdup(ps);
2883  ps = strrchr(ps, '/');
2884  if (ps)
2885  ps++;
2886  else
2887  ps = path;
2888  args[argslen++] = ps;
2889  }
2890  ps = NULL;
2891  i++;
2892  }
2893 
2894 #ifdef USE_NNTP
2895  if (!OptNewsSend)
2896  {
2897 #endif
2898  size_t extra_argslen = 0;
2899  /* If C_Sendmail contained a "--", we save the recipients to append to
2900  * args after other possible options added below. */
2901  if (ps)
2902  {
2903  ps = NULL;
2904  size_t extra_argsmax = 0;
2905  while ((ps = strtok(ps, " ")))
2906  {
2907  if (extra_argslen == extra_argsmax)
2908  mutt_mem_realloc(&extra_args, sizeof(char *) * (extra_argsmax += 5));
2909 
2910  extra_args[extra_argslen++] = ps;
2911  ps = NULL;
2912  }
2913  }
2914 
2915  if (eightbit && C_Use8bitmime)
2916  args = add_option(args, &argslen, &argsmax, "-B8BITMIME");
2917 
2918  if (C_UseEnvelopeFrom)
2919  {
2920  if (C_EnvelopeFromAddress)
2921  {
2922  args = add_option(args, &argslen, &argsmax, "-f");
2923  args = add_args_one(args, &argslen, &argsmax, C_EnvelopeFromAddress);
2924  }
2925  else if (!TAILQ_EMPTY(from) && !TAILQ_NEXT(TAILQ_FIRST(from), entries))
2926  {
2927  args = add_option(args, &argslen, &argsmax, "-f");
2928  args = add_args(args, &argslen, &argsmax, from);
2929  }
2930  }
2931 
2932  if (C_DsnNotify)
2933  {
2934  args = add_option(args, &argslen, &argsmax, "-N");
2935  args = add_option(args, &argslen, &argsmax, C_DsnNotify);
2936  }
2937  if (C_DsnReturn)
2938  {
2939  args = add_option(args, &argslen, &argsmax, "-R");
2940  args = add_option(args, &argslen, &argsmax, C_DsnReturn);
2941  }
2942  args = add_option(args, &argslen, &argsmax, "--");
2943  for (i = 0; i < extra_argslen; i++)
2944  args = add_option(args, &argslen, &argsmax, extra_args[i]);
2945  args = add_args(args, &argslen, &argsmax, to);
2946  args = add_args(args, &argslen, &argsmax, cc);
2947  args = add_args(args, &argslen, &argsmax, bcc);
2948 #ifdef USE_NNTP
2949  }
2950 #endif
2951 
2952  if (argslen == argsmax)
2953  mutt_mem_realloc(&args, sizeof(char *) * (++argsmax));
2954 
2955  args[argslen++] = NULL;
2956 
2957  /* Some user's $sendmail command uses gpg for password decryption,
2958  * and is set up to prompt using ncurses pinentry. If we
2959  * mutt_endwin() it leaves other users staring at a blank screen.
2960  * So instead, just force a hard redraw on the next refresh. */
2961  if (!OptNoCurses)
2962  mutt_need_hard_redraw();
2963 
2964  i = send_msg(path, args, msg, OptNoCurses ? NULL : &childout);
2965  if (i != (EX_OK & 0xff))
2966  {
2967  if (i != S_BKG)
2968  {
2969  const char *e = mutt_str_sysexit(i);
2970  mutt_error(_("Error sending message, child exited %d (%s)"), i, NONULL(e));
2971  if (childout)
2972  {
2973  struct stat st;
2974 
2975  if ((stat(childout, &st) == 0) && (st.st_size > 0))
2976  {
2977  mutt_do_pager(_("Output of the delivery process"), childout,
2978  MUTT_PAGER_NO_FLAGS, NULL);
2979  }
2980  }
2981  }
2982  }
2983  else if (childout)
2984  unlink(childout);
2985 
2986  FREE(&childout);
2987  FREE(&path);
2988  FREE(&s);
2989  FREE(&args);
2990  FREE(&extra_args);
2991 
2992  if (i == (EX_OK & 0xff))
2993  i = 0;
2994  else if (i == S_BKG)
2995  i = 1;
2996  else
2997  i = -1;
2998  return i;
2999 }

◆ mutt_prepare_envelope()

void mutt_prepare_envelope ( struct Envelope *  env,
bool  final 
)

Prepare an email header.

Parameters
envEnvelope to prepare
finaltrue if this email is going to be sent (not postponed)

Encode all the headers prior to sending the email.

For postponing (!final) do the necessary encodings only

3013  {

3015  {
3016  /* some MTA's will put an Apparently-To: header field showing the Bcc:
3017  * recipients if there is no To: or Cc: field, so attempt to suppress
3018  * it by using an empty To: field. */
3019  struct Address *to = mutt_addr_new();

-1Failure

3077  return -1;

3081  struct Buffer *tempfile = mutt_buffer_pool_get();
3082  mutt_buffer_mktemp(tempfile);
3083  FILE *fp_tmp = mutt_file_fopen(mutt_b2s(tempfile), "w");
3084  if (fp_tmp)
3085  {
3086  char date[128];
3087  CopyHeaderFlags chflags = CH_XMIT | CH_NONEWLINE | CH_NOQFROM;
3088 
3089  if (!C_BounceDelivered)
3090  chflags |= CH_WEED_DELIVERED;
3091 
3092  fseeko(fp, e->offset, SEEK_SET);
3093  fprintf(fp_tmp, "Resent-From: %s", resent_from);
3094  fprintf(fp_tmp, "\nResent-%s", mutt_date_make_date(date, sizeof(date)));
3095  char *msgid_str = gen_msgid();
3096  fprintf(fp_tmp, "Resent-Message-ID: %s\n", msgid_str);

3101  fputc('\n', fp_tmp);
3102  mutt_file_copy_bytes(fp, fp_tmp, e->content->length);
3103  if (mutt_file_fclose(&fp_tmp) != 0)

3105  mutt_perror(mutt_b2s(tempfile));
3106  unlink(mutt_b2s(tempfile));
3107  return -1;
3108  }
3109 #ifdef USE_SMTP
3110  if (C_SmtpUrl)
3111  {
3112  rc = mutt_smtp_send(env_from, to, NULL, NULL, mutt_b2s(tempfile),
3113  e->content->encoding == ENC_8BIT);

3116 #endif

3121  }
3122 
3123  mutt_buffer_pool_release(&tempfile);

int mutt_bounce_message ( FILE *  fp,

Bounce an email message.

Parameters
fpHandle of message
eEmail
toAddressList to bounce to
Return values
0Success
-1Failure

3136 {
3137  if (!fp || !e || !to || TAILQ_EMPTY(to))
3138  return -1;
3139 
3140  const char *fqdn = mutt_fqdn(true);
3141  char resent_from[256];
3142  char *err = NULL;
3143 
3144  resent_from[0] = '\0';
3145  struct Address *from = mutt_default_from();
3146  struct AddressList from_list = TAILQ_HEAD_INITIALIZER(from_list);
3147  mutt_addrlist_append(&from_list, from);
3148 
3149  /* mutt_default_from() does not use $realname if the real name is not set
3150  * in $from, so we add it here. The reason it is not added in
3151  * mutt_default_from() is that during normal sending, we execute
3152  * send-hooks and set the realname last so that it can be changed based
3153  * upon message criteria. */
3154  if (!from->personal)
3155  from->personal = mutt_str_strdup(C_Realname);
3156 
3157  mutt_addrlist_qualify(&from_list, fqdn);
3158 
3159  rfc2047_encode_addrlist(&from_list, "Resent-From");
3160  if (mutt_addrlist_to_intl(&from_list, &err))
3161  {
3162  mutt_error(_("Bad IDN %s while preparing resent-from"), err);
3163  FREE(&err);
3164  mutt_addrlist_clear(&from_list);
3165  return -1;
3166  }
3167  mutt_addrlist_write(&from_list, resent_from, sizeof(resent_from), false);
3168 
3169 #ifdef USE_NNTP
3170  OptNewsSend = false;
3171 #endif
3172 
3173  /* prepare recipient list. idna conversion appears to happen before this
3174  * function is called, since the user receives confirmation of the address
3175  * list being bounced to. */
3176  struct AddressList resent_to = TAILQ_HEAD_INITIALIZER(resent_to);
3177  mutt_addrlist_copy(&resent_to, to, false);
3178  rfc2047_encode_addrlist(&resent_to, "Resent-To");
3179  int rc = bounce_message(fp, e, &resent_to, resent_from, &from_list);
3180  mutt_addrlist_clear(&resent_to);
3181  mutt_addrlist_clear(&from_list);
3182 
3183  return rc;
3184 }

◆ mutt_write_multiple_fcc()

int mutt_write_multiple_fcc ( const char *  path,
struct Email *  e,
const char *  msgid,
bool  post,
char *  fcc,
char **  finalpath 
)

Handle FCC with multiple, comma separated entries.

Parameters
[in]pathPath to mailboxes (comma separated)
[in]eEmail
[in]msgidMessage id
[in]postIf true, postpone message
[in]fccfcc setting to save (postpone only)
[out]finalpathFinal path of email
Return values
0Success
-1Failure

Definition at line 3218 of file sendlib.c.

3220 {
3221  char fcc_tok[PATH_MAX];
3222  char fcc_expanded[PATH_MAX];
3223 
3224  mutt_str_strfcpy(fcc_tok, path, sizeof(fcc_tok));
3225 
3226  char *tok = strtok(fcc_tok, ",");
3227  if (!tok)
3228  return -1;
3229 
3230  mutt_debug(LL_DEBUG1, "Fcc: initial mailbox = '%s'\n", tok);
3231  /* mutt_expand_path already called above for the first token */
3232  int status = mutt_write_fcc(tok, e, msgid, post, fcc, finalpath);
3233  if (status != 0)
3234  return status;
3235 
3236  while ((tok = strtok(NULL, ",")))
3237  {
3238  if (*tok == '\0')
3239  continue;
3240 
3241  /* Only call mutt_expand_path if tok has some data */
3242  mutt_debug(LL_DEBUG1, "Fcc: additional mailbox token = '%s'\n", tok);
3243  mutt_str_strfcpy(fcc_expanded, tok, sizeof(fcc_expanded));
3244  mutt_expand_path(fcc_expanded, sizeof(fcc_expanded));
3245  mutt_debug(LL_DEBUG1, " Additional mailbox expanded = '%s'\n", fcc_expanded);
3246  status = mutt_write_fcc(fcc_expanded, e, msgid, post, fcc, finalpath);
3247  if (status != 0)
3248  return status;
3249  }
3250 
3251  return 0;
3252 }

◆ mutt_write_fcc()

int mutt_write_fcc ( const char *  path,
struct Email *  e,
const char *  msgid,
bool  post,
const char *  fcc,
char **  finalpath 
)

Write email to FCC mailbox.

Parameters
[in]pathPath to mailbox
[in]eEmail
[in]msgidMessage id
[in]postIf true, postpone message, else fcc mode
[in]fccfcc setting to save (postpone only)
[out]finalpathFinal path of email
Return values
0Success
-1Failure

Definition at line 3265 of file sendlib.c.

3267 {
3268  struct Message *msg = NULL;
3269  struct Buffer *tempfile = NULL;
3270  FILE *fp_tmp = NULL;
3271  int rc = -1;
3272  bool need_mailbox_cleanup = false;
3273  struct stat st;
3274  char buf[128];
3275  MsgOpenFlags onm_flags;
3276 
3277  if (post)
3278  set_noconv_flags(e->content, true);
3279 
3280 #ifdef RECORD_FOLDER_HOOK
3281  mutt_folder_hook(path, NULL);
3282 #endif
3283  struct Mailbox *m_fcc = mx_path_resolve(path);
3284  bool old_append = m_fcc->append;
3285  struct Context *ctx_fcc = mx_mbox_open(m_fcc, MUTT_APPEND | MUTT_QUIET);
3286  if (!ctx_fcc)
3287  {
3288  mutt_debug(LL_DEBUG1, "unable to open mailbox %s in append-mode, aborting\n", path);
3289  mailbox_free(&m_fcc);
3290  goto done;
3291  }
3292 
3293  /* We need to add a Content-Length field to avoid problems where a line in
3294  * the message body begins with "From " */
3295  if ((ctx_fcc->mailbox->type == MUTT_MMDF) || (ctx_fcc->mailbox->type == MUTT_MBOX))
3296  {
3297  tempfile = mutt_buffer_pool_get();
3298  mutt_buffer_mktemp(tempfile);
3299  fp_tmp = mutt_file_fopen(mutt_b2s(tempfile), "w+");
3300  if (!fp_tmp)
3301  {
3302  mutt_perror(mutt_b2s(tempfile));
3303  mx_mbox_close(&ctx_fcc);
3304  goto done;
3305  }
3306  /* remember new mail status before appending message */
3307  need_mailbox_cleanup = true;
3308  stat(path, &st);
3309  }
3310 
3311  e->read = !post; /* make sure to put it in the 'cur' directory (maildir) */
3312  onm_flags = MUTT_ADD_FROM;
3313  if (post)
3314  onm_flags |= MUTT_SET_DRAFT;
3315  msg = mx_msg_open_new(ctx_fcc->mailbox, e, onm_flags);
3316  if (!msg)
3317  {
3318  mutt_file_fclose(&fp_tmp);
3319  mx_mbox_close(&ctx_fcc);
3320  goto done;
3321  }
3322 
3323  /* post == 1 => postpone message.
3324  * post == 0 => Normal mode. */
3325  mutt_rfc822_write_header(
3326  msg->fp, e->env, e->content, post ? MUTT_WRITE_HEADER_POSTPONE : MUTT_WRITE_HEADER_FCC,
3327  false, C_CryptProtectedHeadersRead && mutt_should_hide_protected_subject(e));
3328 
3329  /* (postponement) if this was a reply of some sort, <msgid> contains the
3330  * Message-ID: of message replied to. Save it using a special X-Mutt-
3331  * header so it can be picked up if the message is recalled at a later
3332  * point in time. This will allow the message to be marked as replied if
3333  * the same mailbox is still open. */
3334  if (post && msgid)
3335  fprintf(msg->fp, "X-Mutt-References: %s\n", msgid);
3336 
3337  /* (postponement) save the Fcc: using a special X-Mutt- header so that
3338  * it can be picked up when the message is recalled */
3339  if (post && fcc)
3340  fprintf(msg->fp, "X-Mutt-Fcc: %s\n", fcc);
3341 
3342  if ((ctx_fcc->mailbox->type == MUTT_MMDF) || (ctx_fcc->mailbox->type == MUTT_MBOX))
3343  fprintf(msg->fp, "Status: RO\n");
3344 
3345  /* mutt_rfc822_write_header() only writes out a Date: header with
3346  * mode == 0, i.e. _not_ postponement; so write out one ourself */
3347  if (post)
3348  fprintf(msg->fp, "%s", mutt_date_make_date(buf, sizeof(buf)));
3349 
3350  /* (postponement) if the mail is to be signed or encrypted, save this info */
3351  if (((WithCrypto & APPLICATION_PGP) != 0) && post && (e->security & APPLICATION_PGP))
3352  {
3353  fputs("X-Mutt-PGP: ", msg->fp);
3354  if (e->security & SEC_ENCRYPT)
3355  fputc('E', msg->fp);
3356  if (e->security & SEC_OPPENCRYPT)
3357  fputc('O', msg->fp);
3358  if (e->security & SEC_SIGN)
3359  {
3360  fputc('S', msg->fp);
3361  if (C_PgpSignAs)
3362  fprintf(msg->fp, "<%s>", C_PgpSignAs);
3363  }
3364  if (e->security & SEC_INLINE)
3365  fputc('I', msg->fp);
3366 #ifdef USE_AUTOCRYPT
3367  if (e->security & SEC_AUTOCRYPT)
3368  fputc('A', msg->fp);
3369  if (e->security & SEC_AUTOCRYPT_OVERRIDE)
3370  fputc('Z', msg->fp);
3371 #endif
3372  fputc('\n', msg->fp);
3373  }
3374 
3375  /* (postponement) if the mail is to be signed or encrypted, save this info */
3376  if (((WithCrypto & APPLICATION_SMIME) != 0) && post && (e->security & APPLICATION_SMIME))
3377  {
3378  fputs("X-Mutt-SMIME: ", msg->fp);
3379  if (e->security & SEC_ENCRYPT)
3380  {
3381  fputc('E', msg->fp);
3382  if (C_SmimeEncryptWith)
3383  fprintf(msg->fp, "C<%s>", C_SmimeEncryptWith);
3384  }
3385  if (e->security & SEC_OPPENCRYPT)
3386  fputc('O', msg->fp);
3387  if (e->security & SEC_SIGN)
3388  {
3389  fputc('S', msg->fp);
3390  if (C_SmimeSignAs)
3391  fprintf(msg->fp, "<%s>", C_SmimeSignAs);
3392  }
3393  if (e->security & SEC_INLINE)
3394  fputc('I', msg->fp);
3395  fputc('\n', msg->fp);
3396  }
3397 
3398 #ifdef MIXMASTER
3399  /* (postponement) if the mail is to be sent through a mixmaster
3400  * chain, save that information */
3401 
3402  if (post && !STAILQ_EMPTY(&e->chain))
3403  {
3404  fputs("X-Mutt-Mix:", msg->fp);
3405  struct ListNode *p = NULL;
3406  STAILQ_FOREACH(p, &e->chain, entries)
3407  {
3408  fprintf(msg->fp, " %s", (char *) p->data);
3409  }
3410 
3411  fputc('\n', msg->fp);
3412  }
3413 #endif
3414 
3415  if (fp_tmp)
3416  {
3417  mutt_write_mime_body(e->content, fp_tmp);
3418 
3419  /* make sure the last line ends with a newline. Emacs doesn't ensure this
3420  * will happen, and it can cause problems parsing the mailbox later. */
3421  fseek(fp_tmp, -1, SEEK_END);
3422  if (fgetc(fp_tmp) != '\n')
3423  {
3424  fseek(fp_tmp, 0, SEEK_END);
3425  fputc('\n', fp_tmp);
3426  }
3427 
3428  fflush(fp_tmp);
3429  if (ferror(fp_tmp))
3430  {
3431  mutt_debug(LL_DEBUG1, "%s: write failed\n", mutt_b2s(tempfile));
3432  mutt_file_fclose(&fp_tmp);
3433  unlink(mutt_b2s(tempfile));
3434  mx_msg_commit(ctx_fcc->mailbox, msg); /* XXX really? */
3435  mx_msg_close(ctx_fcc->mailbox, &msg);
3436  mx_mbox_close(&ctx_fcc);
3437  goto done;
3438  }
3439 
3440  /* count the number of lines */
3441  int lines = 0;
3442  char line_buf[1024];
3443  rewind(fp_tmp);
3444  while (fgets(line_buf, sizeof(line_buf), fp_tmp))
3445  lines++;
3446  fprintf(msg->fp, "Content-Length: " OFF_T_FMT "\n", (LOFF_T) ftello(fp_tmp));
3447  fprintf(msg->fp, "Lines: %d\n\n", lines);
3448 
3449  /* copy the body and clean up */
3450  rewind(fp_tmp);
3451  rc = mutt_file_copy_stream(fp_tmp, msg->fp);
3452  if (mutt_file_fclose(&fp_tmp) != 0)
3453  rc = -1;
3454  /* if there was an error, leave the temp version */
3455  if (rc >= 0)
3456  {
3457  unlink(mutt_b2s(tempfile));
3458  rc = 0;
3459  }
3460  }
3461  else
3462  {
3463  fputc('\n', msg->fp); /* finish off the header */
3464  rc = mutt_write_mime_body(e->content, msg->fp);
3465  }
3466 
3467  if (mx_msg_commit(ctx_fcc->mailbox, msg) != 0)
3468  rc = -1;
3469  else if (finalpath)
3470  *finalpath = mutt_str_strdup(msg->committed_path);
3471  mx_msg_close(ctx_fcc->mailbox, &msg);
3472  mx_mbox_close(&ctx_fcc);
3473 
3474  if (!post && need_mailbox_cleanup)
3475  mutt_mailbox_cleanup(path, &st);
3476 
3477  if (post)
3478  set_noconv_flags(e->content, false);
3479 
3480 done:
3481  if (m_fcc)
3482  m_fcc->append = old_append;
3483 #ifdef RECORD_FOLDER_HOOK
3484  /* We ran a folder hook for the destination mailbox,
3485  * now we run it for the user's current mailbox */
3486  if (Context && Context->mailbox->path)
3487  mutt_folder_hook(Context->mailbox->path, Context->mailbox->desc);
3488 #endif
3489 
3490  if (fp_tmp)
3491  {
3492  mutt_file_fclose(&fp_tmp);
3493  unlink(mutt_b2s(tempfile));
3494  }
3495  mutt_buffer_pool_release(&tempfile);
3496 
3497  return rc;
3498 }

◆ userhdrs_override_headers

const char* const userhdrs_override_headers[]
static
Initial value:
= {
"content-type:",
"user-agent:",
}

The next array/enum pair is used to to keep track of user headers that override pre-defined headers NeoMutt would emit.

Keep the array sorted and in sync with the enum.
